summary: Full-stack browser-based real-time hack-and-slash RPG inspired by Ladder Slasher's gameplay loop. Built with React + TypeScript frontend and Motoko backend on ICP. Features Internet Identity auth, shared persistent on-chain world, ICP-based marketplace, live leaderboard, seasonal resets, hardcore/softcore realms, tick-based combat, class progression with Ascension Trials, and full itemization system.
architectural_notes: Frontend: React + TypeScript with Canvas rendering for dungeon/combat screens
Backend: Motoko canisters on ICP for persistent world state, auth, marketplace, leaderboard, season management
Auth: Internet Identity (passkey-based) tying characters to on-chain accounts
Combat: tick-based (200-500ms) runs client-side; results server-validated on dungeon completion
Modular structure: state/, systems/, ui/, data/ directories for clean separation
Deterministic RNG with seed option for debugging and server-side validation
Season reset triggers automatically when first player completes final class Ascension Trial
Players retain stash/equipment across season resets; class unlock chain resets to Tier 1
known_issues: Real-time WebSocket co-op (live players in same dungeon) is not supported on ICP platform; multiplayer is shared-world async only

existing_features.title: Implement Internet Identity authentication. Users must log in before creating or accessing characters. All on-chain state (characters, inventory, marketplace listings) must be scoped to the authenticated principal.

Implement character creation with two realm choices: Softcore and Hardcore. Each player may have one active character per realm per season. Characters begin at Class Tier 1 (original name: e.g., 'Wanderer'), Level 1, with base stat points to allocate across Strength, Dexterity, Intelligence, and Vitality. Show original in-game descriptions for each stat.

Implement a tick-based combat engine (200–500ms ticks) running client-side. On dungeon completion, submit the combat result to the backend for validation and reward distribution (XP, loot). Combat involves the player and randomly generated monsters; monsters use physical or magical attacks without revealing attack type every round.
Implement 10 original monster types with unique stat profiles (HP, attack, defense, attack speed, loot tables). Monsters are randomly selected and scaled to the current dungeon level.

Implement three dungeon modes: Catacombs (standard pace, full loot to player), Depths (faster pace, higher risk/reward, surprise encounters), and Ascension Trial (the Master Quest gating class tier unlocks). Each mode is selectable from a dungeon selection screen.

Implement leveling and stat allocation. Characters gain XP from combat. On level-up, the player receives stat points to allocate across Strength, Dexterity, Intelligence, and Vitality. Display current stats and their derived effects (damage, speed, HP, defense) on the character sheet.

Implement 8–12 original abilities across melee, ranged, magic, and tank archetypes. Characters may equip up to 3 abilities at a time. Ability points are earned (1 point at start, +1 per 5 levels). Equipped abilities reset when the character rerolls or completes an Ascension Trial.

Implement a loot system with 4 rarity tiers (original names, e.g., Common / Uncommon / Rare / Legendary), randomized affixes (+stats, regen, damage, defense, crit), and item types (weapons, armor, charms/trinkets). Loot drops at dungeon run completion based on monster loot tables and dungeon mode.

Implement an inventory screen with item compare tooltips. Players can equip items to character slots, move items to/from the stash/vault, and view item stats side-by-side with currently equipped items.

Implement an ICP-based marketplace where players can list items for sale at a fixed price or auction, browse listings, and buy items. Cross-player item transfers must be secure and atomic on-chain. Include a safe item vault for holding items intended for transfer.

Implement a live leaderboard showing top players per realm (Softcore and Hardcore), ranked by class tier then level then XP. Hardcore characters who die remain on the current season's leaderboard until the season resets. The leaderboard wipes completely on season reset.

Implement Softcore and Hardcore death mechanics. On Softcore death: apply a penalty (gold loss and/or XP loss) and return the player to the dungeon select screen. On Hardcore death: mark the character as dead permanently, preventing further dungeon runs, but retain the character's stash and keep the character on the current season's leaderboard.

Build all required UI screens: (1) Login/Home screen with Internet Identity, (2) Character sheet with stats, gear slots, and equipped abilities, (3) Dungeon run screen with combat log and visual HP bars, (4) Inventory + stash screen with item compare, (5) Ladder screen with realm tabs, (6) Ascension Trial / Master Quest screen, (7) Marketplace screen with listings, buy/bid, and item vault. Include stub screens for Professions and Shrines marked as 'Coming Soon'.

Apply a cohesive dark fantasy visual theme across all screens: deep stone/obsidian backgrounds, ember and gold accent colors, sharp gothic typography for headings, clean readable body text, subtle particle or glow effects on legendary items and ability icons. The theme should feel like a hand-crafted underground dungeon crawler, not a generic RPG UI.

Fix stat persistence during character creation: when the player allocates 8 stat points and confirms creation, write the allocated Strength, Dexterity, Intelligence, and Vitality values directly into the character's baseStats record in stable storage. Do not use a temporary UI-only state object that gets discarded. Ensure no post-creation initialization step overwrites the assigned values with defaults.

Fix the ability point exploit and rework the ability system: characters start with exactly 1 ability point; earn +1 ability point every 10 levels (so at level 100 a character has 11 total points); each ability purchase costs 1 ability point; block the purchase UI when availableAbilityPoints == 0; enforce a hard cap of 3 simultaneously equipped abilities (players may own more than 3 but only equip 3); expand the ability pool in abilities.ts to approximately 15 abilities spread across melee, ranged, magic, and tank archetypes.

Rebalance monster difficulty: reduce the base damage of all monsters by approximately 10–15% and reduce HP scaling per dungeon level by approximately 10%. Apply these reductions in the monster templates in monsters.ts and in any scaling logic in combatEngine.ts. Keep encounters challenging but smooth out the early progression curve.

Restrict the random item affix pool in lootGenerator.ts to only the following valid stats: Strength, Dexterity, Intelligence, Vitality, +HP, +Physical Damage, +Magic Damage, +Defense, +Critical Chance. Remove all other affix types that are currently generated (e.g. any regen, speed, or unrecognized stats). Apply this restriction to all item types: weapons, armor, and charms/trinkets.

Reduce the global item drop rate by 50% in lootGenerator.ts. This applies to all dungeon modes (Catacombs, Depths, Ascension Trial) and all monster types. If drops are determined by a probability roll, halve that probability. If drops are determined by a count, halve the expected count.

Completely remove the gold system from the entire codebase: (a) backend — remove all gold fields from character/player records in main.mo, remove any gold rewards in dungeon result processing, remove gold from death penalty logic; (b) frontend — remove gold display from CharacterSheet, Navigation, and any other screen; remove gold reward lines from combat/loot output; update the Softcore death penalty to apply XP loss only (no gold loss); remove any vendor or store UI that requires gold. Gear must be self-found or traded via the marketplace only.

new_feature_requests.summary: Shared persistent on-chain world with Internet Identity auth (Motoko backend)
Real-time tick-based combat (client-side, server-validated on completion) with 10 monster types
Character progression: leveling, 4 core stats (Strength/Dexterity/Intelligence/Vitality), stat allocation
8-12 abilities across melee/ranged/magic/tank styles, max 3 equipped, reset on MQ/reroll
Loot system: randomized affixes, 4 rarity tiers, inventory with item compare tooltips
Ascension Trial (Master Quest) encounter gating class tier unlocks; auto season reset on final MQ completion
ICP-based marketplace: list, bid, buy items; cross-player item transfers and item vault/stash
Live leaderboard with Softcore and Hardcore realms; Hardcore characters persist on ladder after death; full wipe on season reset
3+ dungeon modes: Catacombs, Depths (high risk/reward), Ascension Trial dungeon
All UI screens: character sheet, dungeon run, inventory, ladder, MQ/Ascension Trial, marketplace
Professions and shrines as stubbed placeholders for future expansion
Softcore death penalty (gold/progress loss) and Hardcore death (character deleted, stash retained across seasons)

Implement the Motoko backend actor with persistent on-chain storage for: players (principal → character data), characters (stats, class tier, level, XP, realm, alive/dead status), inventory, stash/vault, season number, ladder rankings, and marketplace listings.

Implement the Ascension Trial (Master Quest) system. When a character meets the milestone requirement (level/XP threshold) for their current class tier, they may attempt the Ascension Trial. Completing it unlocks the next class tier. When a player completes the final class tier's Ascension Trial, trigger an automatic global season reset: all characters revert to Class Tier 1 / Level 1; stash and meta progression are retained; the ladder wipes.
